---
title: "About Me"
subtitle: "Background, Skills & Professional Goals"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-title: "On this page"
---

## Who Am I?

I am **Shiva Sagar**, a Business Analytics graduate student at **Dublin City University**, combining a strong quantitative foundation with real-world entrepreneurial and professional experience. My academic journey began with a BA in Business Administration at ICFAI Business School (2024) and has progressed into advanced analytics, machine learning, and AI at DCU.

Before returning to full-time study, I co-founded **Wrench Mate PVT LTD**, where I oversaw product development, financial operations, and multi-channel marketing — an experience that taught me that data alone does not drive decisions; clear communication and strategic thinking do. That insight now shapes every analysis I build.

My core belief: **a great analyst is not just technically skilled — they understand the business context behind every number**.

---

## Education

{{< fa graduation-cap >}} **MSc Business Analytics**
[Dublin City University]{style="font-weight:600; color:#2563eb;"} &nbsp;·&nbsp; Dublin, Ireland &nbsp;·&nbsp; 2025 – Present

Relevant modules:

- Research Methods & Academic Writing
- Analytical Tools and Programming (Python, SQL)
- Statistical Models and Methods of Business Analytics
- Strategic Thinking Tools and Techniques
- Advanced Analytics & AI (NLP, BERT, Deep Learning, LLMs, Generative AI)
- Analytics, Visualisation & Communication (Power BI, A/B Testing, Regression)
- Corporate Strategy and Leadership
- New Venture Strategy & Planning
- AI-Powered Portfolio & Analytics Challenges

{{< fa graduation-cap >}} **BA Business Administration**
[ICFAI Business School]{style="font-weight:600; color:#2563eb;"} &nbsp;·&nbsp; 2024

---

## Technical Skills

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

### Languages & Tools
- {{< fa brands python >}} **Python** — Data analysis, ML, Plotly visualisations
- {{< fa database >}} **SQL** — Data extraction, management, querying
- **Power BI** — Interactive dashboards, KPI tracking
- **Excel** — VLookups, Pivot Tables, advanced formulae
- **Word / Office Suite** — Professional reporting

### Machine Learning & AI
- Classical ML (Scikit-learn: Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost)
- NLP & Transformers (BERT, T5, Hugging Face)
- Unsupervised Learning (K-Means, PCA, LDA, t-SNE)
- Deep Learning & Generative AI / LLMs
- CRISP-DM methodology

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

### Analytics & Statistics
- Descriptive & inferential statistics
- A/B testing and hypothesis testing
- Linear & logistic regression
- Time series analysis
- Dimensionality reduction

### Business & Strategy
- P&L tracking and financial budgeting
- Vendor negotiation and client relations
- PRINCE2 & Data Lifecycle Management
- UN SDG-aligned analytical thinking
- VR-enhanced presentation skills (DCU Lab)

### DevOps & Publishing
- {{< fa brands git-alt >}} **Git & GitHub** — Version control, GitHub Pages
- **Quarto** — Scientific publishing & ePortfolio
- **VS Code** — Primary IDE

:::

:::

---

## Certifications

| Certification | Issuer | Year |
|---|---|---|
| Python for Everybody Specialisation | Coursera / University of Michigan | 2023 |
| Introduction to Machine Learning | Kaggle | 2024 |
| Git & GitHub Foundations | GitHub Education | 2024 |
| Kubicle Data Analytics Training | Kubicle (DCU Partnership) | 2025 |

---

## Professional Goals

> *"My goal is to become a long-term strategic analytics partner to organisations — bringing not just technical capability but genuine business understanding to every data challenge."*

My near-term goals include:

- [x] Build a fully reproducible analytics portfolio with live Python code
- [x] Complete MSc Business Analytics coursework at DCU
- [ ] Secure a graduate analytics or data role from October 2026
- [ ] Obtain professional qualifications in data analytics or business intelligence
- [ ] Lead a client-facing data consultancy project

---

## Values & Approach

::: {.callout-tip title="Reproducibility First"}
Every analysis I build is version-controlled, documented, and fully reproducible. A result you cannot reproduce is a result you cannot trust — especially when business decisions depend on it.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Business Context Matters"}
Having co-founded a startup, I know that technical accuracy means nothing if the insight does not reach the right person in the right format. I always design analyses with the end audience in mind.
:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Ethical Analytics"}
I take algorithmic bias, GDPR compliance, and data privacy seriously — principles reinforced throughout the DCU programme. Responsible analytics is not optional; it is foundational.
:::

---

## Outside of Work

When I'm not building models or dashboards, you'll find me:

- {{< fa rocket >}} Thinking about startup ideas and business models
- {{< fa book >}} Reading about strategy, behavioural economics, and AI ethics
- {{< fa users >}} Networking with peers and professionals in the Dublin analytics community
- {{< fa plane >}} Exploring new places and cultures

---

## Multimedia Highlights

![My analytics workflow — from raw data through modelling to published insight.](images/workflow-diagram.svg){fig-alt="Diagram of data science workflow from raw data through analysis to Quarto publication" fig-align="center" width="80%"}

<div class="ratio ratio-16x9" style="margin: 1.5rem 0;">
  <iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/IsF0yzR4A4s" title="Introduction to Quarto for scientific publishing" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>

*Video: "Getting Started with Quarto" — J.J. Allaire & Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel, rstudio::conf(2022). [Source: YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IsF0yzR4A4s){target="_blank"}.*

---

## Attributions

- Icons provided through the `quarto-ext/fontawesome` extension and Font Awesome Free (SIL OFL 1.1 licence).
- Workflow diagram: original illustration created for this portfolio.
- Video embed: "Hello, Quarto!" keynote, rstudio::conf 2022. Used under YouTube's standard embedding terms.
